title: START_NOT_STICKY prevents phantom notifications — intentional, defend against 'simplification'
description: NieForegroundService returns START_NOT_STICKY from onStartCommand. This means Android will NOT restart the service if it kills it under memory pressure. The relay WebSocket lives in the Flutter/Dart isolate, not in the service. On app return the RelayService reconnects automatically and calls start() again. A contributor might suggest START_REDELIVER_INTENT or START_STICKY to 'be more reliable' — this would be wrong. A restarted service without an active Dart connection would show 'nie is running' with no actual relay connection, which is worse than no notification. The service is a notification wrapper, not the connection owner.
close_reason: Design decision recorded for future reference. No code action required.
